acceptedAnswer.text: INC-22 for same city/RoC. INC-23 for different RoC/state. Additional approvals needed for cross-state changes.

mainEntity.name: How to change registered office within the same city?

acceptedAnswer.text: Pass board resolution. File INC-22 with RoC within 30 days. Provide new address proof (rent agreement + NOC or ownership document) and utility bill. No special resolution needed for change within same RoC jurisdiction.

mainEntity.name: How to change registered office to another state?

acceptedAnswer.text: Requires special resolution (Section 13), application to Regional Director (Form INC-23), newspaper advertisement, and Central Government confirmation. Timeline: 2-4 months. Most complex type of office change.

mainEntity.name: What documents are needed for registered office change?

acceptedAnswer.text: Board resolution, address proof of new office (rent agreement/sale deed), NOC from property owner, utility bill (not older than 2 months), and proof of registered office (INC-22 declaration).

mainEntity.name: Is GST address change needed after registered office change?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. File GST REG-14 for address amendment within 15 days. PAN address update through NSDL. Update with banks, EPFO, ESIC, and all other regulatory registrations.

mainEntity.name: What is the penalty for not intimating registered office change?

acceptedAnswer.text: Penalty of ₹1,000 per day of default under Section 12(8) of the Companies Act, 2013. Maximum: ₹1 lakh. Every officer in default also liable.
INC-22 for same city/RoC. INC-23 for different RoC/state. Additional approvals needed for cross-state changes.
